creator: dewayne@heuristicsystems.com.au
text:
(In reply to Marcin Cieślak from comment #4)
Hi Marcin, yes Cy's fix is for the base only.  Perhaps its time to add to security/heimdal 
BROKEN_FreeBSD_14= fails to kinit # or something more appropriate

To the workarounds.
-------------------
I've appended the suggestion to /etc/krb5.conf
https://bugs.freebsd.org/bugzilla/show_bug.cgi?id=275915#c1 which also appears at
https://bugs.freebsd.org/bugzilla/show_bug.cgi?id=276415

[kadmin]
    default_keys = aes256-cts-hmac-sha1-96:pw-salt
which doesn't help.  

FYI I also have in krb5.conf
[libdefaults]
  default_etypes = aes256-cts-hmac-sha384-192 aes256-cts-hmac-sha1-96

I then applied https://github.com/heimdal/heimdal/issues/1224#issuecomment-1897866938
to /etc/ssl/openssl.cnf and /usr/local/etc/ssl/openssl.cnf (as ktrace indicates use of the later) and for good measure defined OPENSSL_CONF

openssl_conf = openssl_init

[openssl_init]
providers = provider_sect

[provider_sect]
default = default_sect
legacy = legacy_sect

[default_sect]
activate = 1

[legacy_sect]
activate = 1

still unsuccessful. (I guess magic doesn't happen sometimes).

To your question
----------------
# ldd `which openssl` /usr/local/bin/openssl |grep -e ^/ -e crypt
/usr/bin/openssl:
        libcrypto.so.30 => /lib/libcrypto.so.30 (0x8011f6000)
/usr/local/bin/openssl:
        libcrypto.so.12 => /usr/local/lib/libcrypto.so.12 (0x8011ef000)

# ldd /usr/bin/kinit /usr/local/bin/kinit|grep -e ^/ -e crypt
/usr/bin/kinit:
        libcrypto.so.30 => /lib/libcrypto.so.30 (0x801117000)
        libcrypt.so.5 => /lib/libcrypt.so.5 (0x801951000)
/usr/local/bin/kinit:
        libhcrypto.so.4 => /usr/local/lib/heimdal/libhcrypto.so.4 (0x801251000)
        libcrypto.so.12 => /usr/local/lib/libcrypto.so.12 (0x80133d000)
        libcrypt.so.5 => /lib/libcrypt.so.5 (0x80173f000)
Note libhcrypto :)

Hail Mary
---------
Rebuilding security/openssl with LEGACY=ON and reinstalling, the error is

# /usr/local/bin/kinit dewayne
dewayne@HS's Password:
kinit: aes-256: EVP_CipherInit_ex einit

Conclusion and future
---------------------
I suspect the issue is that heimdal isn't ready for openssl 3.0.x(?) or I'm missing an incantation...

Cy has addressed the urgent need to get base heimdal functioning and its unrealistic to expect him to apply the patches to security/heimdal, as this is something that the heimdal authors should address. Unfortunately neither 7.8.1 nor 8.0 appear to be on the horizon.  So after using heimdal 0.6.3 through all the ports versions (and accessed via dovecot, sendmail, ldap, mod_auth_kerb2 etc), its necessary to migrate to krb5. The carrot for the migration is the availability of FAST (already in samba416 lorikeet, which is an updated heimdal) and SPAKE :}.

Please let me know if there's anything else, as I'll take a day or so to plan the move (for krb5 as server).

FYI: Please refer to 
https://bugs.freebsd.org/bugzilla/show_bug.cgi?id=277650#c18
where Cy notifies the intention to move heimdal to MIT's Krb5 in base.
